titleStmt.title: Memorandum by Colonel J. W. Middleton to Lieutenant General Alvan C. Gillem, Jr.

head.hi: Memorandum by Colonel J. W. Middleton to Lieutenant General Alvan C. Gillem, Jr.

div.p:
The approved directive to Executive Headquarters contains a statement that each organization to be retained in the Chinese Army will institute a twelve (12) weeks’ basic training program. It is believed that such a program can best be prepared by the group now in Nanking under General McClure.Maj. Gen. Robert B. McClure, acting head of the Military Advisory Group being organized at Nanking. It is suggested the following radio be transmitted:
“To: Commanding General, Nanking Headquarters Command.
Request you prepare a 12 weeks’ basic training program for units of Chinese Army to be retained after demobilization. This program is
for interim training to keep troops occupied during period in which they have nothing else to do and must be capable of being operated entirely by Chinese. Request such a program be delivered to Chungking in one week. Colonel McClure,Col. Clinton I. McClure, U. S. A. of your headquarters, is familiar with the requirements.
Signed, Gillem.”
